subject: Health Education
preamble: This examination syllabus is drawn from the Senior School Health Education curriculum and directed towards empowering students to have correct, adequate and timely information that will make them acquire attitude and practices which will restore, maintain and promote positive health.
aims_and_objectives: acquire basic knowledge of the human body and necessary skills for maintenance of health;
acquire positive health practices in the school and community;
identify and show the ecological relationship between man and his environment as a basis for preventing diseases;
make observations and draw inferences from practical experiences that may have implications for health;
develop readiness necessary for professional training in health-related careers.

papers.description: Will consist of fifty multiple choice objective questions, all of which must be taken in 1 hour for 50 marks.
The paper will last 1 hour and carry a total of 70 marks.

sections.instructions: Will consist of six short-structured questions, all of which must be answered for 30 marks.
Will consist of three essay questions out of which candidates will be required to answer two questions for 40 marks.

papers.description: Will be a practical test for school candidates and an alternative test of practical work for private candidates. The paper will last for 1 3/4 hours and carry 80 marks.

detailed_syllabus.title: History and Development of Health Education
topics: Meaning and setting for health education (home-based, school-based, community-based, health facility-based, work place-based)

detailed_syllabus.title: Human Anatomy and Physiology
topics: Cells and tissues of Human Body (somatic and sex cells, cell division, cell differentiation, growth and development)
Sense Organs (structure and functions of eye, ear, skin, nose, tongue)
Systems of the Body (skeletal and muscle systems, circulatory system, digestive system, excretory system, respiratory system, nervous system, endocrine system, homeostasis)
Posture and Postural Defects (definition, characteristics, determination, types, and effects)

detailed_syllabus.title: Personal Health
topics: Meaning and importance of physical health
Health Screening (eye defects, auditory defects, skin disorders)
Dental health education (types of human teeth, teeth diseases)

detailed_syllabus.title: Community Health
topics: Community health services (definition, providers, community efforts)
School health programme (definition, components, goals, objectives, and promotion linkages)
Family health (meaning, aspects like maternal and child health, family size and budgeting)

detailed_syllabus.title: Environmental Health
topics: Housing (criteria, components, effects of poor sanitation)
Water supply (definition, sources, purification, and uses)

detailed_syllabus.title: Nutrition and Food Nutrients
topics: Nutrition (classes, sources, functions of food nutrients, caloric value)
Diet for different groups of people

detailed_syllabus.title: Safety Education and First Aid
topics: First Aid (meaning, aims, principles, kit, specific emergency conditions, agencies)

detailed_syllabus.title: Drug, Alcohol and Tobacco Education
topics: Drug Education (terminologies, classes of drugs, ways of taking drugs, drug abuse)

detailed_syllabus.title: Communicable and Non-Communicable Diseases
topics: Communicable diseases (definition, classification: air-borne, water/food-borne, insect-borne, worm infection, animal-borne, contact diseases, factors necessary to occur)

detailed_syllabus.title: Family Life and Human Sexuality Education
topics: Human Reproduction (definitions, structures, functions, conception, pregnancy, disorders)
Human Sexuality Education (meaning, importance, life skills)

detailed_syllabus.title: Emotional and Social Health
topics: Mental health (definition, promotion, types, prevention, importance)

detailed_syllabus.title: Consumer Health Education
topics: Factors influencing choice of consumer products

materials_needed: Models of various organs of the body
Models of various parts of the body e.g. tooth
Reagents for testing of starch, protein, fat
Posters/charts of systems of the body
Photographs of health facilities and workers
Photographs of methods of sewage and refuse disposals
Photographs/posters of various types of communicable and non-communicable diseases
Model/poster showing parts of the body
Photographs/posters showing methods of food preservation
Real objects/photographs/poster/charts of classes of food
Poster/real object of sphygmomanometer, thermometer, litmus paper, snellen chart
